coin_profile.name: YLDS
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: YLDS (YLDS) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #103 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.7/10 and survivability 56.7%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -29.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.7/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-29.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 56.7% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 1 method contributed to the median — the valuation should be treated as a rough anchor, not a precise fair value. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

bull_points: Current market fear environment may create a near-term mean reversion opportunity in YLDS
Any positive news catalyst — exchange listing, partnership, or protocol upgrade — could trigger a sharp near-term rally from current levels
Crypto market sentiment is cyclical — YLDS's short-term setup may improve as macro uncertainty fades
bear_points: Broader crypto market volatility and macro headwinds present near-term downside risk
Regulatory developments in major markets could create short-term selling pressure
Without a near-term catalyst, YLDS may continue to consolidate or drift lower

bull_points: Over 100% of the maximum supply is already in circulation — future dilution risk is minimal for long-term holders
As the crypto ecosystem matures, YLDS's established position should compound in value
Continued development activity and ecosystem growth support a constructive long-term view
bear_points: No multi-year fee data exists for YLDS — the protocol's long-term economic durability remains unproven
Technology or regulatory disruption risk cannot be ruled out over a 3-5 year horizon
Long-term execution risk remains a key variable for any crypto investment

quality_narrative.classification_reason: YLDS carries a Dangerous rating because its fundamentals score only 4.7/10 as a Cash-Flow Protocol, with notable weaknesses in network demand. Dangerous-rated tokens carry a higher risk of permanent capital loss and should only be held with strict position sizing and a clear exit plan.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: YLDS is a Cash-Flow Protocol with weak fundamentals, scoring 4.7/10 overall. Its clearest strength is dilution control, while network demand remains the main area to watch. Key risks: low network demand score (1/10). YLDS warrants caution and should only be held as a small position with a clear thesis and defined risk limits.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: YLDS scores 4.7/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.name: yearn.finance
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: yearn.finance (YFI) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #322 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: YFI has a weak protocol moat despite perfect dilution control; it faces intense competition from forked yield aggregators (Yearn clones exist across multiple chains) with no sustainable differentiation, and token utility is limited as governance capture by core team reduces holder value accrual. While ecosystem lock-in exists through vault deposits and developer integrations, the low fee growth (4/10) and network demand (4/10) signals indicate declining competitive relevance, and the protocol's maturity score (1/10) suggests outdated architecture relative to emerging yield strategies.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

bull_points: <cite index="1-1,1-3">A major September 2025 governance proposal from 0xPickles to redirect 90% of protocol revenue to YFI stakers and overhaul tokenomics could trigger sharp upside if the community vote passes, directly linking token value to cash flows</cite>.
<cite index="1-7,1-8,1-9">Yearn's partnership with Katana as infrastructure provider for their March 2026 TGE marks a major ecosystem expansion that could drive vault adoption and fee growth</cite>.

<cite index="5-3,5-4">Recent launches of auto-compounding vaults and OEV-Boosted ETH strategies combined with upcoming Katana and Term Labs integrations provide near-term catalysts to attract meaningful deposit inflows</cite>.
bear_points: <cite index="1-17,1-18">A November 2025 exploit in Yearn's legacy yETH stableswap pool drained ~$9M and exposed ongoing security risks in legacy code, potentially deterring new deposits despite the core vaults remaining unaffected</cite>.

<cite index="5-7,5-8,5-9">Convex Finance's 62% capture of Curve voting power and Pendle Finance's 240% YTD TVL growth in tokenized yield products pose direct competitive threats to Yearn's fee-dependent revenue model on ~$4B TVL</cite>.
<cite index="1-6">The proposed governance reform risks failure if the community vote does not pass, leaving Yearn's incentive structure and token value proposition unaligned</cite>.

bull_points: <cite index="1-2,1-3,1-5">The September 2025 tokenomics overhaul proposal to scrap the underused vote-escrow model and focus DAO on profitability is bullish long-term because it could directly link token value to protocol revenue, incentivizing sustainable value accrual for long-term holders</cite>.

<cite index="1-14,1-17">With five years of successful DeFi integrations and partnerships (Katana, Liquity, Alchemix, Curve), Yearn's established position in the maturing yield optimization ecosystem provides structural competitive advantages and network effects that compound over years</cite>.

<cite index="1-2">At 97.41% circulating supply already in circulation (FDV/MC ratio of 1.03), YFI has minimal future dilution risk, protecting long-term token holders from supply-side pressure over a multi-year horizon</cite>.
bear_points: <cite index="1-17,1-18,1-20,1-21">The November 2025 yETH exploit demonstrates that legacy DeFi code carries persistent security risks that could repeat, underscoring structural execution and operational risk that cannot be ruled out over a 3-5 year horizon</cite>.

<cite index="5-9,5-10">Yearn's long-term fee sustainability is unproven given competitive pressure from Convex (62% Curve CRV power) and Pendle (18.7% fixed-rate alternatives)—without 20%+ APY advantages, market share erosion will pressure the protocol's 1.5% fee revenue model</cite>.

Without multi-year fee data demonstrating durable cash flows, the protocol's long-term economic model remains unvalidated; regulatory disruption or technological obsolescence risk cannot be discounted over a 5-year investment horizon.
